inputs.price_source: Williams-Sonoma IR stock quote 2026-07-08 (pre-mechanical refresh; marvin_cloud_refresh updates spot)
inputs.fcf_source: FY2025 cash from operations $1,314.9M minus capital spending $259.4M = $1,055.5M free cash flow divided by 123.2M diluted weighted-average shares (10-K filed 2026-03-26, fiscal year ended 2026-02-01)
inputs.shares_source: FY2025 weighted average diluted shares (10-K FY2025)
inputs.dividend_source: Cash dividends declared $2.64 per share in fiscal 2025; quarterly dividend raised 15% in March 2026 (10-K, 8-K 2026-03-18)
inputs.normalization_note: Lawrence base uses FY2025 filed free cash flow. FY2025 included one fewer week versus fiscal 2024 (~$117M revenue headwind per 10-K). Q1 FY2026 company comparable brand revenue +4.8% on net revenue +4.4%; not used as FCF starting point.

bear.notes: Housing and discretionary spend soften; brand comps turn negative; tariff and freight pressure on gross margin; buybacks slow as comps weaken

base.notes: Mid-single-digit company comp on omnichannel strength; Pottery Barn and West Elm grow modestly; continued buybacks and dividend; operating margin ~18%

bull.notes: Sustained furniture and kitchen momentum (Williams Sonoma +6.9% FY2025 brand comp); e-commerce mix stays above 65%; aggressive capital return continues

option_scan.question: GAAP book misstates core assets?
option_scan.evidence: Retail inventory, leased stores, and distribution assets at GAAP; no land NAV misstatement (10-K FY2025)

option_scan.question: Undeveloped reserves / dormant assets?
option_scan.evidence: Operating omnichannel home retailer; no resource royalty

option_scan.question: In-business loss segment?
option_scan.evidence: All reportable brand segments profitable; operating income $1.416B FY2025 (10-K segment note)

option_scan.question: Backlog / embedded product option in revenue path?
option_scan.evidence: Business-to-business and franchise revenues embedded in brand segments; The Key Rewards loyalty program drives repeat purchase (10-K)

option_scan.question: Private stakes below fair value?
option_scan.evidence: No material private equity stakes on balance sheet (10-K)

option_scan.question: Transitory legal recovery?
option_scan.evidence: Operating compounder; no dated litigation recovery

option_scan.question: Embedded product option already in revenue?
option_scan.evidence: Emerging brands Rejuvenation, Mark and Graham, GreenRow flow through All Other segment revenue; modeled via growth not separate terminal (10-K)

stance_proposal.rationale: Mechanical Lawrence synthesis below 15% hurdle at premium multiple on FY2025 owner cash despite strong comp trends and capital return

growth_explanation.mechanism: Company comparable brand revenue growth from omnichannel furniture, kitchen, and collaboration assortments across Pottery Barn, West Elm, and Williams Sonoma; partially offset by store closures and franchise revenue decline.
growth_explanation.filing_anchor: FY2025 company comp +3.5%; Q1 FY2026 company comp +4.8% (10-K FY2025, Q1 FY2026 10-Q)
growth_explanation.bear_trigger: Housing turnover and tariff-driven gross margin compression push brand comps negative for four quarters.
growth_explanation.bull_trigger: Sustained furniture cycle and Williams Sonoma kitchen strength lift company comp above 5% while buybacks shrink share count 3%+ annually.
